sourceLabel: Lexicon IELTS and Spoken English Institute admissions and demo

entries.title: Admission and level-assessment process
entries.content: The admission process usually begins with a short counselling call or walk-in discussion to understand whether the learner wants IELTS Academic, IELTS General, spoken English, or communication support. For many learners, Lexicon also does a short level assessment before placing them in a suitable batch, then shares the recommended programme, timing, and payment steps.

entries.title: Demo and trial policy
entries.content: Demo or orientation sessions are available in planned slots, but they are not treated as unlimited free classes on demand. Spoken English enquiries may sometimes get a short trial interaction, while IELTS enquiries are often given a structured counselling-plus-demo slot so the learner can understand teaching style, batch pace, and mock-test format before admission.

entries.title: Callback support
entries.content: If a learner asks for a call, Lexicon's counselling desk usually tries to return the call between 10:30 AM and 7:00 PM. Same-day callbacks are attempted where possible, but the front desk should not promise an exact minute unless a counsellor has already confirmed the slot. Learners can share their preferred call time and whether they want guidance in Hindi or English.
